license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yf2ftqe
title: PBMC Isolation
author: Dannielle DK Moore

steps_raw.instruction: In a Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific decant 15 mL of Ficoll-Paque PLUS density gradient media GE Healthcare Catalog #17144002

In a separate Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific dilute peripheral blood in 1:1 ratio with 1x Phosphate-Buffered Saline Lonza Catalog #04-479Q to a max volume of 35mL (max starting volume of blood is ~18mL) Note: if a larger volume of blood is required - the blood must be split across seperate falcon tubes and isolated PBMCs combined following isolation procedure, prior to cell counting
Gently layer the diluted blood from step 2 onto the Ficoll from step 1.
Following layering, secure and remove tubes from hood.
Following centrifugation, steralize tubes and return to hood for further processing.

Wash isolated PBMCs twice in 20 mL 1x Phosphate-Buffered Saline Lonza Catalog #04-479Q Centrifuge cell suspension at 400xg for 00:10:00 at 23 °C, (Room temp) with the brake and accelerator set to max.

Dilute cells in 1:10 ratio with 1x Phosphate-Buffered Saline Lonza Catalog #04-479Q and Trypan Blue Solution 0.4% Sterile-filtered Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #T8154 Count the cells using haemocytometer and microscope. Record observed cell number and cell viability

Use a sterile Pasteur pipette to carefully remove (and discard) the upper plasma layer until 5cm above opaque PBMC band. In a circular motion, collect the PBMC band at the Ficoll interface and transfer into a new Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific Note: if a large volume of blood was processed and blood split into several tubes, collect and decant all PBMC bands into single tube.
Centriguge at 400xg for 00:25:00 at 23 °C, (Room temp) with the accelerator and brake OFF.
